Petition Of Nelson H. Wild, Guardian Ad Litem For Dr. Frank Hinman, Jr. For Fees And Costs, And For Discharge.
Off Calendar Aug-08-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nelson Wild in Propria Persona as Guardian Ad Litem for Dr. Hinman.

The Court stated that it has no problem with the petition. Mr. Wild stated that he did not received any opposition. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition Of Nelson H. Wild, Guardian Ad Litem For Dr. Frank Hinman, Jr., For Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Nelson Wild, Guardian Ad Litem for Frank Hinman. Sidney Luscutoff for Robert Dreyer.

Mr. Wild and Mr. Zabronsky stated that the reason for the request for Mr. Wild to remain on was for the purpose of signing the taxes, for dealing with the issue regarding Dr. Hinman's care and for looking out for Dr. Hinman's interest. Mr. Luscotoff stated that he would be filing a petition to restore status of the Trustee. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Approval Of Settelement Agreement.
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for American Urological Association. Nancy Riley in Propria Persona. Nelson Wild Guardian Ad Litem for Dr. Hinman. Sidney Luscotoff for Robert Dreyer (CPA). Michell Trausch for Regents University of California and John Hopkins University.

The Parties presented oral argument/expressed their concerns with regard to the settlement agreement. Upon conclusion of argument, The Court granted both petitions (subject to the written orders).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Continued To Dec-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle Trausch for Regents of University of California and John Hopkins University. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Petitioner). Nelson Wild, Guardian Ad Litem for Frank Hinman.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that Mr. Carr's client did not sign the settlement agreement and that her objections had not been withdrawn. The Court directed Mr. Wild to file his report and the parties to file there responses. Mr. Wild stated that he spoke to Dr. Maybury and Dr. Sandhu, that they agreed as to Mr. Hinman's medical condition, stated that Mr. Hinman made no indication as to visiting the Tahoe property, and that Mr. Hinman was frail and that he had difficulty walking. In addition, he stated that he had no specifics as to when the Family was taking Mr. Hinman to Tahoe or as to how long, that any visit would have to be approved by his Doctors and that the potential buyer had agreed to allow Mr. Hinman access to the property. The Court stated that it did not want Mr. Wild to file the report if there was success with Mr. Carr and Ms. Lewis. The Court will give them one week's time. If there is no agreement, Mr. Wild is to file his report by November 6, 2009 and the responses are due November 17, 2009. The matter was continued to December 7, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the agreement is signed, then the Petition For Instructions can go off calendar and the Petition To Approve The Settlement can be set for December 7, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Vacated

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Continued To Oct-19-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. Michelle Trausch for the Regents of UC and Johns Hopkins University. John Carr for Sally Lewis. Richard Fogg (Trustee) in pro per.

Mr. Zabronsky stated that the parties had reached a settlement regarding the removal which encompasses this petition, that Ms. Lewis' objections will be withdrawn, that the Guardian ad Litem's (GAL) authority is ok, and that he can file a report. Ms. Trausch stated that Mr. Wilde had a conflict, that he would not be appearing today, that the settlement will be subject to Court approval and will be circulating this week, in the mean time there will be an interim order which will allow the GAL to go forward with his report, and that the sale issue should be put over two weeks. Mr. Carr stated that they had settled and the remainder of the today's petition should be put over two weeks and then there will be a noticed petition to approve the settlement. The Court signed the interim order in open court and continued the matter to October 19, 2009. Petition For Instructions Re Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Aug-27-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: EDWARD KOPLOWITZ, APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;JOHN CARR APPEARING FOR SALLY LEWIS;MICHELE
TRAUSCH, APPEARING FOR UCSFF & JOHN HOPKINS; ERIC BEHRENS APPEARING FRO UCSFF; DON FEILDS APPEARING FOR AND WITH M. BRINKMANN AND R. DRYER.

MR. CARR STATES THE TWO CAREGIVERS (BRINKMANN & DRYER) DRAFTED THE AMDENDMENT TO THE TRUST AGREEMENT, THEN HAD THE TRUSTOR SIGN. LATER TAKING THE ADMENDMENT TO AN ATTORNEY. HE WISHES THAT THE POWERS OF BRINKMANN AND DRYERS BE SUPENDED AND THAT MS. LEWIS SERVE WITH WELLS FARGO BANK AS TEMPORARY TRUSTEES. MR. KOPLOWITZ AND MS. TRAUSCH AGREES WITH MR. CARR. MR. CARR HAS NO KNOWLEDGE OF THE FILING OF AN EXTENTION OF THE TAXES OR OF APPRIASIAL ON THE PROPERTIES. MR. FIELD STATES THAT THE TRUSTEES HAVE ISSUES THAT ARE NOT RESOLVED, BEGINNING WITH THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A TRUST ACCOUNT. COUNSEL ALSO STATES THAT MR. DRYER HAS FILED FOR AN EXTENSION ON THE TAXES. COUNSEL WILL AGREE TO SUSPEND THE POWERS OF BRINKMANN AND DRYER PROVIDING AN OUTSIDE FINANCIAL INSTITUTION IS APPOINTED. MR. KOPLOWITZ STATES THAT THE ADMENDENT WAS SIGNED TWO DAYS BEFORE THE TRUSTOR(DR. HINMAN) AND THAT THE TRUSTOR WOULD HAVE LEFT A SUBSTANTIAL AMOUNTS TO THE MEDICAL FIELD AND THE AMENDMENT DOSE NOT, BUT THAT THE ORIGINAL TRUST DOES. COUNSEL STATES THAT MS. BRINKMANN HAS NO EXPERTIES AS A TRUSTOR. ALSO THAT MS. LEWIS IS A FAMILY MEMBER AND HAS HANDEING THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRUST ALONG WITH WELLS FARGO BANK. THE COURT SUSPENDS THE POWERS OF MS. BRINKMANN AND MR. DRYERS ON A TEMPORARY BASIS AND APPOINTS WELLS FARGO AND MS. LEWIS AS CO-TRUSTEES. MR. TO PREPARE ORDER.
JUDGE: JHN DEARMAN;REPORTER:KATY BERSAMIN,CSR#11722; CLERK:LARRISA DOTSON;BALIFF: M. LOOS
